text: Here's a video of a robotic ventral hernia repair with mesh.
The surgeon begins the robotic ventral hernia repair by dissecting the hernia sac after safely accessing the intra-abdominal cavity.
The hernia defect is exposed and sized, followed by the closure of the defect using robotic instruments.
The surgeon places an adequately sized mesh into the intra-abdominal cavity and secures it to the abdominal wall with a central stitch.
The surgeon uses a series of transfascial sutures and circumferential tacks to securely attach the mesh to the abdominal wall, ensuring stable fixation within the intra-abdominal cavity.
